title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Gate Lens: Repo Purpose
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates repo purpose increment in a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Repo Purpose
steps.instruction:

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set. This fails the Repo Purpose contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Repo Purpose evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-repo-purpose-1qxexm: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Repo Purpose into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-repo-purpose-1qxexm:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Repo Purpose failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Repo Purpose, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Repo Purpose model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Repo Purpose applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
